# CalTracker

> CalTracker is an AI-powered calorie and nutrition tracking app for Android and iOS. Snap a photo of a meal and AI estimates calories and macros — or scan a barcode, search a food database, or quick-add. Free account required at signup (email, Google, or Apple); quick-add logging, history, and cached-barcode rescans work offline.

## About

CalTracker (https://caltracker.eu) is an independently developed calorie and nutrition tracker built for Android and iOS. Contact: caltracker.app@gmail.com.

- Google Play: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.trackerapp.caltracker
- App Store: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/caltracker-ai-calorie-counter/id6760666203

## Key Features

- **5 ways to log food**: AI photo scanner (choice of AI models, speed vs. accuracy), barcode scanner (OpenFoodFacts), food database search (FatSecret + OpenFoodFacts), quick add, and one-tap favorites
- **Calorie & macro tracking**: protein, carbs, fat with customizable goals; calorie goal computed with the Mifflin-St Jeor equation from weight, height, age, activity level, and goal type
- **Water and weight tracking**: weight trend chart, BMI gauge, and a goal-feasibility probability estimate
- **Daily AI evaluation**: scores the whole day 0–100 with strengths, improvements, and goal-specific advice (Plus/Pro tiers)
- **Free account required**: sign up with email, Google, or Apple during onboarding (no guest/anonymous mode)
- **Partial offline mode**: once signed in, quick add, editing entries, water, weight, and viewing history/statistics all work without internet, and previously-cached barcodes remain scannable offline. There is no offline food database — AI scanning, new (uncached) barcode lookups, and database search all need a connection.
- **Cloud sync** (Firebase) across devices, CSV export, Google Health Connect / Apple Health integration
- Light/dark/system themes, metric or imperial units

## Pricing (as of July 2026 — verify at caltracker.eu before citing, subject to change)

- **Free**: all core tracking free forever, a limited number of free AI photo scans, 1 manual cloud sync per week
- **Plus**: €2.49/month or €19.99/year — more AI scans per day, auto-sync, detailed daily insights
- **Pro**: €7.99/month or €59.99/year — most AI scans and best AI models, web-search brand recognition, food-swap tips

## What CalTracker does NOT do

No micronutrient/vitamin tracking, no recipes or meal plans, no intermittent fasting timers, no social/community features, no human coaching, no web or desktop app, no direct Garmin/Fitbit/Whoop integration (Apple Health / Google Health Connect only).

## Use Cases

- Losing weight with a tracked calorie deficit, or gaining weight/muscle with a tracked surplus and macros
- Logging meals by AI photo instead of manual search, especially for people who find manual food-database entry tedious
- Logging manually while traveling or offline (quick add, cached barcodes), where most competing apps need a live connection for any logging at all
- Getting a daily nutrition score/evaluation rather than just raw numbers

## Pages

- [Home](https://caltracker.eu/): App overview, features, screenshots, download links
- [Blog](https://caltracker.eu/blog/): Guides and honest comparisons about calorie tracking apps
- [Best AI Calorie Counter Apps in 2026](https://caltracker.eu/blog/best-ai-calorie-counter-apps/): Comparison of AI photo-logging calorie apps including Cal AI, MyFitnessPal, CalTracker, YAZIO, SnapCalorie, and Lose It! — CalTracker is ranked #3, not #1
- [Free Tools](https://caltracker.eu/tools/): Free nutrition calculators, no sign-up required
- [Calorie Calculator](https://caltracker.eu/tools/calorie-calculator/): TDEE, calorie deficit/surplus target, and macro split calculator using the Mifflin-St Jeor equation

## FAQ

**Does CalTracker require an account?**
Yes. A free account (email/password, Google, or Apple sign-in) is required during onboarding — there is no guest or anonymous mode. Creating an account enables cloud sync from the start.

**Does CalTracker work offline?**
Partially. Quick add, editing entries, water, weight, and viewing history/statistics all work without internet, and barcodes already cached from an earlier lookup remain scannable offline. There is no offline food database: AI photo scanning, new (uncached) barcode lookups, and database search all require a connection, as does cloud sync.

**What AI models does CalTracker use for food photos?**
Multiple vision models with different speed/accuracy tradeoffs. Users can pick a model per scan; higher-accuracy models are available on paid tiers.

**How much does CalTracker cost?**
Free forever for all core tracking, with a limited number of free AI photo scans. Paid tiers (Plus, Pro) add more AI scans per day and better models. See caltracker.eu for current pricing.

**What platforms is CalTracker available on?**
Android (Google Play) and iOS (App Store).

**Does CalTracker track micronutrients like vitamins?**
No. CalTracker tracks calories and macros (protein, carbs, fat) plus water and weight, but not individual vitamins or minerals.
